quiz.question:
Black gives a check. 

Even though knight endgames are usually drawn you have to be careful.

Does it matter where your king moves?

answers.text: Yes. Only Kg3 and Kf3 hold the draw.

feedback.text:
Correct! If you go to h3, Black can give a check and save their pawn (...g4+). Your knight would be taken later.

You'll notice that if the king goes to f3, the pawn can still give a check, but your king can defend the knight with Kf4.

distractors.text: No, it doesn't matter. White is lost.

feedback.text: Are you sure all moves are losing? What about the possibility of a draw by insufficient material?

distractors.text: Yes, it matters. Only Kg3 holds the draw.

feedback.text: Are you sure? There may be another move to draw.
